threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look confused when the bus driver doesn't wait for you to sit down before pulling away. Classic newbie move.
Spotted a guy in a full chicken costume singing 'Don’t Stop Believin’' on the 88 last night. Brixton never sleeps, not even in fancy dress.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a busker playing accordion outside the Kiosk while a group of kids danced in the midst of traffic. Only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Friedrichshain, there are still places where you can find a club inside an old public swimming pool. Nothing beats dancing in the water echoes.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local when they know to avoid 16th Street BART during rush hour, but newcomers just stand there like deer in headlights.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Last week, saw a guy in a full tuxedo serenading Muni passengers with a ukulele. Only in SF do you get live music with your commute.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think they can find good paella everywhere, but real locals know it’s a coastal dish. Here in Gràcia, it's all about the tapas.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they still ask for 'tapas' instead of 'pica-pica'. They’re still counting on the menu, too.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think you can rely on the Tube to get anywhere on time. Good luck with that during a strike!
Last night on the 196, a guy serenaded us with Coldplay as we crawled past the Ritzy. Only in Brixton do you get a live gig for the fare.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I saw a kid riding a bicycle with a cat in the front basket, I realized Neukölln had seeped into my soul.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do people calmly smoke outside train stations while waiting for delayed buses during a strike, as if it’s all part of the daily routine.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by their hasty hop over those manhole covers—only tourists stand still to snap a pic of the street art nearby.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus today right as a guy in a superhero cape ran to catch it. He missed, but the driver stopped to let him on anyway.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists always think paella is a staple here. But in Gràcia, you’re more likely to find a plate of ‘bomba’ than a seafood dish.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You’ll see them still clutching that paper map, staring bewilderedly at the metro signs while locals zip past, earbuds in, no second glances.
